things.name: Palacerigg Country Park
things.description: Large country park on the edge of Cumbernauld with a nature reserve, golf course, children's farm and moorland walks

things.name: Antonine Wall (UNESCO World Heritage)
things.description: The northern frontier of the Roman Empire built in AD 142 — the best-preserved section at Bar Hill Fort passes through Cumbernauld

things.name: The Falkirk Wheel (Day Trip)
things.description: The world's only rotating boat lift — an extraordinary feat of engineering connecting Scotland's two great canals

things.name: The Kelpies (Day Trip)
things.description: Andy Scott's magnificent 30-metre horse head sculptures in Falkirk's Helix Park — the world's largest equine statues

things.name: Cumbernauld 1960s New Town Architecture
things.description: Cumbernauld's radical modernist town centre is an internationally studied example of 1960s Brutalist urban planning

things.name: Stirling Castle (Day Trip)
things.description: One of Scotland's grandest royal castles — home of the Stuart monarchs with a spectacular Renaissance Palace

things.name: Glasgow (Day Trip)
things.description: Scotland's largest city just 30 minutes by bus — world-class museums, galleries, Mackintosh buildings and great food

things.name: Drumpellier Country Park
things.description: Popular country park with two lochs and woodland walks — a tranquil escape close to Cumbernauld

things.name: Kilsyth Heritage Museum
things.description: Local heritage museum in the nearby market town of Kilsyth with displays on the 1645 Battle of Kilsyth and local history

things.name: Summerlee Museum (Day Trip)
things.description: Scotland's noisiest museum — working trams, a reconstructed mine and ironworks celebrating Lanarkshire's industrial heritage
